canonical_or_religious_significance: One of the 108 Divya Desam temples
historical_significance: Temple constructed in Dravidian style with Vijayanagara architecture. Vimana gilded with gold donated by Sri Krishna Deva Raya in 1517. Richest temple in world in terms of donations received. Located at 3,200 feet altitude on Tirumala Hills in Eastern Ghats.

canonical_or_religious_significance: One of the 275 Paadal Petra Sthalams; one of the Pancha Sabhai where Shiva performed cosmic dance
historical_significance: Temple mentioned in Tamil texts from 6th century CE. Early structures built during Pandya dynasty 12th-13th century CE. Rebuilt by Vijayanagara Empire in 14th century after destruction by Delhi Sultanate. Extensively expanded by Madurai Nayaks 16th-17th centuries. Spread over 5.7 hectares with 14 gopurams, tallest being southern tower at 170 feet built late 16th century.
approx_established_period: 6th century CE (earliest mention), present structure 12th-17th century CE

canonical_or_religious_significance: One of the twelve Jyotirlinga temples; one of the 275 Paadal Petra Sthalams; one of the Char Dham pilgrimage sites
historical_significance: In devotional tradition, lingam established by Rama before crossing to Lanka. Expanded during 12th century by Pandya Dynasty. Sanctum renovated by Jaffna kingdom monarchs. Temple has longest corridor among all Hindu temples in India built by King Muthuramalinga Sethupathy. Contains 22 theerthams representing 22 arrows of Rama. Present structure believed built during 17th century.

7th century Shiva temple located in Hampi on banks of Tungabhadra River. Part of Group of Monuments at Hampi designated UNESCO World Heritage Site. Temple expanded by Lakkan Dandesha under ruler Deva Raya II of Vijayanagara Empire. Uninterrupted history from 7th century CE. Krishna Devaraya commissioned central pillared hall in 1510 CE. Only well preserved temple in Hampi among numerous temples destroyed by Bahmani Sultanate.

canonical_or_religious_significance: One of the 108 Divya Desam temples
historical_significance: Temple mentioned in Sangam period literature, Vishnu Purana, Brahma Purana and other Hindu texts. According to tradition, established in 9th century. Major renovation conducted by Anizham Thirunal Marthanda Varma, idol reconsecrated in 1731 CE. Idol made from 12,008 shaligrams. In 1750, king dedicated kingdom to deity in Thrippadidanam ceremony. Tutelary deity of Travancore royal family. Temple vaults discovered in 2011 contain vast treasures estimated worth billions.

Constructed by Chola emperor Rajaraja I between 1003-1010 CE. One of three Great Living Chola Temples designated UNESCO World Heritage Site. Temple tower 216 feet high, tallest of its kind. Complete structure made of granite. Features 13-foot-high Nandi statue carved from single rock. Exemplary specimen of Dravidian architecture.

In devotional tradition, site where Ayyappa meditated after defeating demoness Mahishi. World's largest annual pilgrimage site attracting around 50 million devotees yearly. Temple surrounded by 18 hills. Pilgrims ascend 18 sacred steps called Pathinettam Padi. Major pilgrimage season during 41-day Mandala kaalam and Makaravilakku festival. Accessible only on foot via 4-kilometer trek from Pamba. Pilgrims required to undergo 41-day fasting before pilgrimage.

canonical_or_religious_significance: One of the 108 Abhimana Kshethram of Vaishnavism
historical_significance: According to devotional tradition, temple established by Guru and Vayu, wind god. Current central shrine rebuilt in 1638 CE. Idol is four-armed form of Vishnu approximately 1.2 meters tall carved from rare black mineral Panchajanya. Features 70-foot-tall golden Dwajasthamba. Known as Bhuloka Vaikunta. Temple features traditional Kerala architecture with copper-sheeted roofs plated with gold.

canonical_or_religious_significance: First and foremost of the 108 Divya Desam temples
historical_significance: World's largest functioning Hindu temple. Features majestic gopurams and centuries-old architecture. Major pilgrimage center for Vaishnavism.

canonical_or_religious_significance: One of the 275 Paadal Petra Sthalams; one of the Pancha Sabhai where Shiva performed cosmic dance
historical_significance: In devotional tradition, site where Shiva performed Ananda Thandava. Temple features five principal halls or Sabhas. Houses 108 karanas (dance postures) of Natya Shastra depicted in sculptures. Annual Natyanjali Festival attracts classical dance artists worldwide.

canonical_or_religious_significance: One of the twelve Jyotirlinga shrines; considered by devotional tradition to be a Shakti Peetha; various texts cite differing numbers of Shakti Peethas
historical_significance: Temple situated on flat top of Nallamalai Hills approximately 300 meters above sea level on right side of Krishna River. One of most ancient kshetras in India. Significant to both Shaivism and Shaktism traditions.

Temple established in 13th century by Sri Madhvacharya, founder of Dvaita school of Vedanta. According to devotional tradition, idol found by Madhvacharya inside large ball of sandalwood. Unique feature: idol faces west contrary to normal practice. Traditional Kerala architecture with red tiles. Famous for Paryaya festival where administration rotates among eight mathas.

Built during reign of Pallava king Rajasimha/Narasimhavarman II in 7th-8th century CE. One of oldest temples of significance in South India. Part of Group of Monuments at Mamallapuram designated UNESCO World Heritage Site. Built of cut stones and granite blocks reflecting Dravidian architecture. Pyramidal Kutina-type tower. 2004 tsunami exposed old collapsed temple built of granite blocks. According to devotional tradition, one of Seven Pagodas of Mamallapuram with six believed submerged in sea.

canonical_or_religious_significance: One of the 108 Divya Desam temples
historical_significance: One of principal Vishnu temples in Kanchipuram. Features 32 shrines, 19 vimanams, 389 pillared halls with elaborate yali sculptures, and sacred tank Anantha Theertham. Notable for 96 ornate sculpted pillars depicting legends of Mahabharata and Ramayana. Main idol brought out for darshan once every 40 years from temple tank. Important additions made during Vijayanagara Empire 13th-14th century.

canonical_or_religious_significance: One of the Pancha Bhoota Sthalas representing earth element
historical_significance: Temple houses thousand-pillared hall built during Vijayanagara period. Also contains Nilathingal Thundam Perumal shrine, one of the Divya Desams. Major pilgrimage site in Kanchipuram representing earth among five elements.

canonical_or_religious_significance: One of the 108 Divya Desam temples
historical_significance: Temple situated on Simhachalam Hill Range 300 meters above sea level. Only temple in India where Varaha Lakshmi Narasimha Swami, combination of third and fourth incarnations of Vishnu, appears. Deity appears in tribhanga posture with two hands, lion head on human torso. Temple embellished with detailed stone carvings.

canonical_or_religious_significance: One of the 108 Divya Desam temples
historical_significance: Temple located 21 km northwest of Madurai on picturesque Alagarmalai hills. Surrounded by ruins of historic fort. Main tower believed built by Pandyan Kings with sculptures depicting scenes from epics. Kalyana Mandapa features beautiful Nayaka art. Main deity called Paramaswamy, processional idol called Alagar. During Chithirai festival, in devotional tradition, Alagar visits sister Meenakshi's wedding.

canonical_or_religious_significance: One of the 275 Paadal Petra Sthalams
historical_significance: Ancient temple in Mylapore, Chennai featuring vibrant Dravidian architecture. Major pilgrimage site in Chennai with significant cultural importance.

canonical_or_religious_significance: One of the 275 Paadal Petra Sthalams; one of the Pancha Bhoota Sthalas representing fire element
historical_significance: Temple famous for massive gopuram. One of largest temple complexes in India. Located at foot of Arunachala hill. Major pilgrimage destination during Karthigai Deepam festival.
Masterpiece of Hoysala architecture built during 12th century. Famous for intricate stone carvings and sculptural excellence. One of finest examples of Hoysala temple architecture in Karnataka.

Ancient coastal shrine famous for Atmalinga legend. Major pilgrimage site combining spiritual significance with scenic coastal surroundings. Pilgrims visit for rituals while temple located near famous beaches like Om Beach.

Temple in Sringeri blending Vijayanagara and Hoysala architectural styles. Features intricately carved stone pillars with astronomical alignment marking equinoxes. Located in Western Ghats offering peaceful environment. Built in 1338 CE by Vidyaranya. Part of Sringeri Sharada Peetham, which has a tradition dating from 8th century.
Temple features one of tallest Shiva statues in world at 123 feet. Located on coast of Arabian Sea with scenic views. Major pilgrimage and tourist destination in Karnataka.

Famous for hosting over one million small Shivalingas along with massive central Shivalinga. One of largest temple complexes in Karnataka. Devotees come for poojas and blessings. Expansive temple grounds offer peaceful contemplative experience.
Part of Hampi UNESCO World Heritage Site. Famous for musical pillars and stone chariot. Exemplary Vijayanagara architecture. One of most iconic structures in Hampi complex.

Major Durga temple in Vijayawada located on Indrakeeladri hill on banks of Krishna River. Important pilgrimage site especially during Navaratri festival. One of most visited temples in Andhra Pradesh.

canonical_or_religious_significance: One of the Pancha Bhoota Sthalas representing air element
historical_significance: Ancient temple representing air among five elements. Famous for Rahu-Ketu puja. Located on banks of Swarnamukhi River. Major Shaivite pilgrimage site.
approx_established_period: 5th century CE (earliest known), major construction 10th-12th century CE

historical_significance: Ancient Shiva temple in Thrissur considered among oldest temples in Kerala. Classic example of Kerala style architecture. Temple hosts famous Thrissur Pooram festival. Important cultural and spiritual center.
Major Bhagavathy temple in Kerala famous for healing powers. Devotees believe deity cures mental ailments. Important pilgrimage site near Ernakulam. Goddess worshipped in three forms throughout day.
Ancient Shiva temple famous for murals and traditional Kerala architecture. Known for Arattu festival. Located near Kottayam. Important pilgrimage site with rich cultural heritage.

canonical_or_religious_significance: One of the 108 Divya Desam temples
historical_significance: Ancient Vishnu temple in Triplicane, Chennai built by Pallavas in 8th century. One of oldest temples in Chennai. Features unique processional deity with mustache. Important architectural and cultural monument.

canonical_or_religious_significance: One of the 275 Paadal Petra Sthalams
historical_significance: Ancient temple famous for Sathabhishekam ceremony for 80th birthday. Associated with saint Abhirami Bhattar who composed Abhirami Andhadhi. Major pilgrimage site for longevity rituals.

canonical_or_religious_significance: One of the 275 Paadal Petra Sthalams
historical_significance: Ancient temple associated with Mahamaham festival held once every 12 years. Located in temple town of Kumbakonam. Major architectural and pilgrimage site.

canonical_or_religious_significance: One of the six Arupadaiveedu temples of Murugan
historical_significance: One of six major abodes of Murugan. Temple located atop hill requiring climb of 365 steps. Ancient pilgrimage site with connection to Vallimalai marriage legend. Important Murugan worship center.

canonical_or_religious_significance: One of the six Arupadaiveedu temples of Murugan
historical_significance: One of six major abodes of Murugan. Temple famous for Panchamirtham prasadam. Located on hilltop accessible by steps or winch. Major pilgrimage center attracting millions annually.

canonical_or_religious_significance: Principal worship center of Ayyavazhi
historical_significance: Primary pilgrimage center of Ayyavazhi faith. In devotional tradition, birthplace of Ayya Vaikundar. Important religious site for Ayyavazhi followers in South India.

Unique temple where deity represents trinity of Brahma, Vishnu and Shiva. Features musical pillars and massive Hanuman statue. Located near Kanyakumari. Important example of confluence of worship traditions.
Major Narasimha temple in Telangana located on hill. Important pilgrimage site near Hyderabad. Temple underwent major renovation and expansion in recent years.

Major Rama temple in Telangana on banks of Godavari River. In devotional tradition, place where Rama and Sita stayed during exile. Famous for Sri Rama Navami celebrations. Temple rebuilt during 17th century by Kancharla Gopanna (Bhakta Ramadasu).
Major Shaiva monastery and educational institution. One of important Adhinams in Tamil Nadu. Center of Shaiva Siddhanta philosophy and Tamil culture.

canonical_or_religious_significance: According to devotional tradition, original seat (Moolasthanam) of Padmanabhaswamy
historical_significance: Only lake temple in Kerala. According to devotional tradition, original spiritual seat before Padmanabhaswamy Temple Thiruvananthapuram. Temple has vegetarian crocodile as guardian. Located in Kasaragod district.
Ancient temple located at confluence of Neyyar River, Karamana River and Arabian Sea. Unique location at estuary makes it significant pilgrimage site.

Masterpiece of Hoysala architecture built during 12th century in Halebidu. Famous for intricate stone carvings depicting Hindu mythology. Construction began around 1121 CE under Hoysala king Vishnuvardhana. Temple features detailed sculptures on walls.
Ancient temple in Mangalore from which city derives its name. Important Shakti temple in coastal Karnataka. Features Kerala style architecture.

Major Shakti temple in Karnataka located in Western Ghats. Important pilgrimage center. In devotional tradition, place where Adi Shankaracharya installed Sri Chakra. Temple attracts devotees from across South India.
Ancient Shakti temple in Dakshina Kannada district on small island in Nandini River. Important pilgrimage site in coastal Karnataka. Temple features traditional Kerala-Karnataka architectural style.

Major Subrahmanya temple in Karnataka located in Western Ghats. Famous for Sarpa Samskara (serpent worship rituals). Important pilgrimage center especially for Rahu-Ketu doshas. Temple situated among Kumaradhara River.
Ancient Ganesha temple in heart of Thiruvananthapuram city. One of oldest temples in capital. Major cultural and religious landmark.
Ancient Vishnu temple in Pathanamthitta district. One of oldest temples in Kerala. Important pilgrimage center with rich traditions and festivals.

Major Bhagavathy temple in Thiruvananthapuram famous for Attukal Pongala festival. Festival holds Guinness World Record for largest annual gathering of women for religious activity. Millions of women participate in Pongala ritual.
